strengths: 19+ years of diverse software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, exceeding the 15-year requirement
Strong technical fundamentals in software craftsmanship, architecture, DevOps, and infrastructure (TDD, microservices, Kubernetes, AWS CDK)
Demonstrated entrepreneurial leadership as GISCollective founder and organizer of technical workshops and courses
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML expertise or products built with AI; lacks Master's/PhD in AI-related field explicitly preferred for this AI Strategy role
14 months of experience with AI-agent infrastructure tooling (MCP) appears tooling-focused rather than demonstrating deep AI/ML domain expertise
No evidence of strategic advisory work with major customers, executive partnerships, or C-suite engagement expected at director-level IC seniority

key_factors.factor: Missing core AI/ML specialization for AI Strategy principal role
Exceeds experience threshold with strong technical and infrastructure foundation
14-month tenure in AI-adjacent work shows recent pivot but insufficient depth documentation

response.justification: Szabo is an exceptionally capable software engineer with nearly two decades of diverse technical experience and genuine craftsmanship discipline. However, this role is explicitly focused on AI Strategy—requiring recognized AI/ML expertise and demonstrated products built with AI. The resume shows recent engagement with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, but provides no evidence of AI/ML domain depth, machine learning systems knowledge, or strategic AI thinking. While the strong engineering foundation could serve as a launchpad, the absence of the advertised specialization represents a fundamental mismatch for a principal-level AI strategist.
